title: Civilian toll mounts as US–Iran exchange raises questions of proportionality
snippet: Human rights groups warn that urban strikes risk breaching international humanitarian law.
raw_text: Reporting from Tehran and Geneva, this newspaper documents hospital admissions and displacement figures that independent monitors say are climbing faster than official tallies. Legal scholars interviewed here argue that collective punishment and threats to cultural sites sit uneasily with UN Charter commitments. The editorial line stresses diplomacy, de-escalation, and renewed IAEA access rather than further militarisation of the Gulf.

title: Editorial: The Iran crisis needs diplomacy, not another endless war
snippet: A negotiated freeze on enrichment and shipping-lane protections should be the priority.
raw_text: The widening US–Iran confrontation cannot be solved from the air alone. We welcome European and regional efforts to reopen channels to Tehran while safeguarding Strait of Hormuz traffic. Labour unions and migrant-worker advocates remind us that South Asian seafarers bear disproportionate risk; any kinetic spiral will hit the global poor first. Sanctions relief tied to verifiable safeguards remains the progressive path.

title: Decisive western strikes restore deterrence after Iran’s provocations
snippet: Officials say calibrated force sent an unmistakable signal to proxies and planners in Quds.
raw_text: Senior defence analysts quoted in this report describe the operation as restoring credibility to red lines that had blurred after months of drone harassment and rocket fire. The government’s friends abroad are applauding resolve; markets in Mumbai initially wobbled then steadied as oil futures priced in a shorter conflict. National security voices argue that hesitation only emboldens adversaries—India should deepen intelligence sharing with partners who keep sea lanes open.

title: Iran’s proxy network on the back foot as logistics hubs are disrupted
snippet: Military sources claim storage and command sites used by militias have been degraded.
raw_text: Coalition briefings highlight a succession of precision hits that reportedly degraded missile workshops and drone launch rails relied on by Tehran-aligned militias. Business leaders interviewed welcome a tougher posture they say protects energy investments; commentators aligned with the ruling dispensation frame the conflict as civilisation versus chaos. The piece emphasises domestic political dividends for leaders who project strength and minimise public vacillation.

title: US–Iran flare-up: oil shippers brace for volatile week in the Gulf
snippet: Freight insurers widen war-risk clauses; Indian imports largely hedged for now.
raw_text: Tanker brokers and insurers in Dubai and Singapore told this newspaper that premiums are climbing but cargoes continue to move. Indian refiners report diversified sourcing and strategic reserves sufficient for several weeks. The article lays out three scenarios—short flare, contained blockade, and wider regional war—with GDP impact estimates and quotes from former diplomats urging hotlines between militaries to avoid miscalculation.

title: Fact-check: what open-source imagery really shows at targeted Iranian sites
snippet: Satellite analysts disagree on extent of damage; IAEA has not verified several viral claims.
raw_text: This centrist desk compares government statements, think-tank releases, and commercial satellite passes. Some craters align with known facilities; others appear on dual-use industrial parks where harm to civilians is harder to assess. The piece interviews a physicist and a former safeguards inspector, balances hawkish and dovish interpretations, and ends with a timeline of what inspectors would need to re-enter the country.

title: Climate crisis: rich nations still short on the $100 bn promise, activists say
snippet: Grassroots movements demand debt swaps and grants, not more loans tagged as climate aid.
raw_text: Environmental justice organisers from island states and South Asia tell this newspaper that adaptation money remains a trickle compared to loss-and-damage needs after successive cyclones. The story foregrounds labour rights in renewable supply chains and criticises fossil fuel expansion disguised as transitional gas. It calls for progressive taxation on wealth and aviation to fund public green jobs.

title: Arctic melt accelerates; scientists say worst-case pathways are back on the table
snippet: New ice-core and satellite synthesis points to feedback loops outpacing older models.
raw_text: Interviews with climatologists stress equity: those who did least to cause heating face inundation and glacial lake outbursts. The Hindu’s coverage ties international mitigation to domestic coal-phase debates and argues publicly owned grids should prioritise affordable clean power for farmers and small industry, not only urban elites and data centres.

title: Energy security first: why climate deals must allow India room to grow
snippet: Commentators say binding western targets should not throttle emerging economies’ baseload needs.
raw_text: The piece platforms voices who argue national development cannot pause for abstract global targets drafted in wealthy capitals. It praises domestic nuclear and hydro expansion, criticises activist litigation that delays mines, and suggests market-led tech transfer beats donor lectures. Climate concern is framed through jobs, strategic autonomy, and affordable power for manufacturing champions.

title: Green rules must not stifle MSME competitiveness, industry tells EU buyers
snippet: Exporters seek phased compliance as Brussels tightens carbon border measures.
raw_text: Representatives of engineering clusters say Brussels’ carbon border adjustment is protectionism with a climate label. The article backs government pushback at WTO and argues Indian firms should not bear disproportionate audit costs while competitors enjoy subsidies masked as green incentives. Growth-first framing pairs with selective praise for domestic solar manufacturing incentives.

title: Global South says climate finance pledges remain opaque ahead of summit
snippet: OECD figures and NGO tallies diverge; ministers ask for a single transparent ledger.
raw_text: Reporters tally conflicting definitions of what counts as climate aid. Finance ministry officials and NGO analysts are quoted side by side; charts show flows by region. The story neither lionises nor dismisses COP processes—it lays out procedural fixes and notes which G20 members missed reporting deadlines.

title: Arctic science update: what changed since the last IPCC cycle
snippet: Researchers agree on direction of travel; debate continues on how fast albedo loss compounds warming.
raw_text: A plain-language walkthrough of recent papers, with cautious language on single-study hype. Includes a sidebar on insurance markets and reinsurance exposure. Ends with policy-neutral bullet points: what diplomats should watch at the next plenary, without prescribing a domestic political course.

title: At WTO, India joins call to discipline rich-country farm subsidies
snippet: Developing bloc says domestic support entrenching Northern agribusiness hurts small growers worldwide.
raw_text: Geneva correspondents describe a united push for special safeguard mechanisms and cotton-sector reforms. The piece highlights solidarity with African and Latin American co-complainants and criticises corporate capture of trade negotiating texts. It argues public stockholding for food security should be shielded from litigation.

title: Colombo seeks Indian investment as IMF program steadies finances
snippet: Ports, power and tourism deals on the table; security cooperation quietly expanded.
raw_text: The report welcomes closer economic integration under a neighbourhood-first doctrine. It stresses opportunities for Indian firms, strategic pushback against rival lenders, and stability after years of turmoil. Commentary frames regional leadership as both moral and commercially savvy.

title: UN Security Council split on humanitarian corridors in active conflict zones
snippet: Veto patterns unchanged; smaller states table compromise language on pauses and aid inspection.
raw_text: A straightforward account of speeches, amendments, and abstentions. Perm-five positions are summarised without editorial endorsement; Indian diplomats’ talking points are reported as delivered. Brief historical comparison to prior resolutions on similar crises.

title: E20 petrol rollout: two-wheeler owners cite mileage drop, seek clearer advisories
snippet: Consumer groups want transparent testing data and support for mechanics in informal sector.
raw_text: Ground reporting from Chennai and Pune documents uneven fuel quality and confusion at bunkers. Environmentalists welcome lower aromatics but stress public transport investment cannot be replaced by ethanol blending alone. The story amplifies unions and commuter associations asking for subsidies for retrofits and stronger grievance redress.

title: Ethanol push strains water tables in sugarcane belts, farmers warn
snippet: Researchers call for diversified feedstock and MSP reforms to avoid monoculture stress.
raw_text: The article connects E20 targets to agricultural political economy: who benefits from price guarantees and who bears groundwater costs. It advocates procurement transparency and help for dryland growers not swept into cane. Editorial sympathy lies with smallholders and ecological limits over headline blending percentages.

title: E20 fuel a self-reliance milestone, says Petroleum Minister
snippet: Officials highlight forex savings and farm linkage; oil PSUs report smooth supply switch.
raw_text: The government-centric narrative celebrates blending targets as strategic autonomy reducing import bills. Business voices predict downstream investment in flex-fuel components. Critics of naysayers appear briefly as ‘vested interests’; the dominant frame is national achievement and energy security aligned with a strong executive push.

title: Oil marketing companies firm up E20 expansion timeline across zones
snippet: Retail network upgrades on track; automakers release compatibility bulletins for new models.
raw_text: A market-forward piece quoting OMC executives and OEM labs. Tone is optimistic about logistics and standardisation, with efficiency framed as consumer convenience once initial adjustments pass. Minimal space for environmental caveat; emphasis on execution discipline and investor confidence.

title: E20 petrol: what changed at the pump since January
snippet: A region-by-region look at ethanol percentages, octane labels, and dealer training.
raw_text: Service-station audits and transport department circulars are compared. The article quotes mechanics, company helplines, and one dissatisfied rider alongside a satisfied fleet operator. Box explains chemistry in simple terms; no single political camp is favoured.

title: Ethanol economics: the cane-versus-maize debate in key states
snippet: State revenues, crop rotations, and distillery capacity differ; no one-size answer, say experts.
raw_text: A data-led comparison of procurement patterns and distillery locations. Political reactions from state agriculture ministers are reported neutrally. Conclusion lists unresolved questions for the next inter-ministerial review rather than a verdict on the policy itself.

title: Assembly elections: civil society presses for real-time disclosure of campaign money
snippet: Watchdogs flag opaque bonds successor instruments and uneven EC enforcement.
raw_text: On the trail in multiple states, reporters document rallies funded by murky pools and digital ads micro-targeting communities. The Hindu foregrounds labour organisers and student groups demanding level playing fields and media accountability. Coverage is sceptical of muscular majoritarian messaging and highlights local issues—wages, healthcare, land—often absent from prime-time packaging.

title: Assembly polls: parties differ sharply on minority representation in candidate lists
snippet: Activists say tokenism persists; some regional outfits project more inclusive slates.
raw_text: Constituency-level analysis shows where alliances brokered seat-sharing and where incumbents face anti-defection churn. Interviews with voters from diverse backgrounds explore safety, hate speech, and policing. The piece advocates constitutional values and social harmony over polarisation as electoral strategy.

title: PM leads blitz in key states as party projects development double engine
snippet: Roadshows draw massive crowds; rivals struggle to match narrative on welfare delivery.
raw_text: Breathless colour from helicopter hops and stadium speeches. The ruling formation is cast as disciplined, patriotic, and responsive to aspirational voters. Opposition fragmentation receives analytical blame for predicted outcomes; law-and-order successes and infrastructure ribbon-cuttings dominate the frame.

title: Star campaigners and seat math: who gains from nationalist pitch on the ground
snippet: Analysts say security-first messaging resonates in swing belts; local caste equations still decisive.
raw_text: Psephology meets on-the-ground colour: which rallies moved needle districts. The tone treats strong leadership and decisive ideology as electoral assets. Cultural motifs and security narratives are described as effective without much critical distance; business leaders’ endorsements feature prominently.

title: Assembly elections: phase-wise voter turnout trends and what they may mean
snippet: Urban pockets lag rural; women voters up in several segments compared to 2021.
raw_text: ECI data tables drive the story, with cautious interpretation from psephologists who disagree on signal versus noise. Local issues—water, jobs—surface in vox pops presented without editorial ranking of parties.

title: Assembly polls: affidavit scan shows criminal cases still common across major parties
snippet: ADR-style tally compares 2021 and 2026; independents and regional parties not exempt.
raw_text: Machine-readable data from candidate affidavits summarised into charts. Parties receive proportionate scrutiny; quotes from election watchdog lawyers explain legal categories. No endorsement of a ‘clean’ camp—readers get facts and context.

title: RBI holds rates; critics say joblessness and rural demand need fiscal support
snippet: Monetary stance alone cannot fix consumption collapse in informal sector, economists argue.
raw_text: The analysis centres working-class distress: stagnant wages, gig precarity, and health expenditure shocks. It calls for expanded welfare, urban employment guarantees, and progressive taxation rather than sole reliance on rate cuts. Markets coverage is secondary to household balance sheets.

title: RBI signals confidence as inflation eases; equities cheer steady guidance
snippet: Governor’s statement highlights macro stability; brokerage notes see room for growth pickup.
raw_text: Investor-focused write-up emphasising policy credibility, forex buffers, and capex cycle optimism. Social impact of rates gets a short nod; dominant chord is that disciplined macro management positions India for a global growth premium narrative.

title: RBI policy: five charts on credit growth, deposits, and transmission
snippet: Retail loans cool slightly; transmission to new borrowers faster than older floating rates.
raw_text: Charts sourced from RBI bulletins with brief explanations. Quotes from two banks with opposing takes on liquidity. Ends with calendar of next inflation prints and MPC meetings—useful reference without political spin.
